findings.evidence: CyclicInterval.lean docstring: 'These lemmas are deliberately stated at the one-dimensional Fin n level.' cIoo is defined there (CyclicInterval.lean:47); cIoo_image_rev depends only on mem_cIoo and Fin.rev.
findings.fix: Move cIoo_image_rev into TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/CyclicInterval.lean (adding Mathlib.Data.Fin.Rev there), alongside disjoint_cIoo_swap and the other cIoo bookkeeping.
findings.issue: Grid.cIoo_image_rev is a pure one-dimensional cIoo/Fin.rev fact with no rotation or rectangle dependency; its canonical home is CyclicInterval.lean, which exists precisely to collect cIoo lemmas 'deliberately stated at the one-dimensional Fin n level'.

findings.evidence: transpose lives in Rectangle.lean:235/634, Complex.lean:52 (transposeEquiv), BlockedRectangle.lean:27 (count_transpose), DifferentialSymmetry.lean (fullyBlockedDifferential_transpose); DifferentialSymmetry.lean is titled 'Symmetries of the fully blocked grid differential' and already houses the two sibling symmetries.
findings.fix: Distribute to canonical homes mirroring transpose: GridRectangle.rotate / GridRectangleBetween.rotate / rotateEquiv / isEmpty_rotate / avoidsMarkings_rotate → Rectangle.lean; GridChain.rotateEquiv → Complex.lean (next to transposeEquiv); fullyBlockedRectangleCount_rotate + mem_fullyBlockedRectangles_rotate → BlockedRectangle.lean; fullyBlockedDifferential_rotate + ...OnGenerator_rotate → DifferentialSymmetry.lean.
findings.issue: The whole rotation stack is placed in a new RectangleRotation.lean, but the established convention splits each symmetry across existing files: the transpose analogues of every declaration here live in Rectangle.lean (rectangle ops + isEmpty/avoidsMarkings), Complex.lean (GridChain.transposeEquiv), BlockedRectangle.lean (fullyBlockedRectangleCount_transpose), and DifferentialSymmetry.lean (fullyBlockedDifferential_transpose). Splitting rotation off scatters the differential symmetries and separates GridChain.rotateEquiv from transposeEquiv, harming discoverability.

summary: The rotation symmetry is consolidated into one new file, but the codebase develops each grid symmetry across a fixed five-file stack; every rotation declaration has a transpose twin whose canonical home is an existing earlier file. Imports are correct.
